params.name: Marsoulas Conch Horn
params.description:
On May 14, 1931 AD, archaeologists discovered this horn in the Maltravieso cave in the Pyrenees.
Initially, people thought this seashell was used as a container. However, after re-examination eighty years later, scientists found some artificial traces on it, including dye and protective coating, and thus finally determined that this seashell was a horn from 18,000 years ago, and was considered the world's oldest musical instrument.
